problem_description: Traditional summative assessments can no longer provide a reliable picture of student understanding due to widespread generative AI usage. HEPI's 2025 survey revealed how 88% of UK students now use AI for assessments, fundamentally comprising the validity of coursework, essays and take-home exams. This creates what VivaEdu determines the "Perfect Papers, Empty Minds" phenomenon as described anecdotally by professors where students are able to produce sophisticated work without truly understanding what they've produced. As we've witnessed firsthand, for example, a recent Durham graduate shared how they heavily used AI during their dissertation writing, then realised upon their presentation prep for the dissertation that they didn't fully understand their own work and had to effectively relearn it in order to be presentation-ready.            Therefore the core problem is that educators need authentic assessment which gives a complete picture of genuine understanding as written work reflect an inauthentic picture of a student's understanding.  Whereas Vivas are able to fully reflect a student's honest understanding of material, but have been historically impossible to scale across large undergraduate cohorts due to time and resource constraints.

solution_explanation: VivaEdu uniquely addresses the scalability problem of utilising vivas to address generative AI into undergraduate cohorts. Our comprehensive platform enables educators to create and distribute a complete viva assessment to their entire class within minutes using our AI-assistant question builder and streamlined setup wizard. program_goals: Through gaining a place on the programme we aim to conduct rigorous studies with UCL's Knowledge Lab to demonstrate how VivaEdu's oral assessment format promotes authentic understanding and deeper learning compared to traditional written assessments. Enabling the academic credibility for the VivaEdu scalable solution. ai_problem_solving: VivaEdu addresses the escalating crisis of cognitive offloading in higher education. HEPI's recent survey shows 88% of UK students now use AI for assessments and frequent AI use correlates significantly with reduced critical thinking skills (Gerlich 2025). This reveals a concerning paradox: students must be AI-literate for future employment prospects, yet educators need to ensure their students do not become AI-dependent. VivaEdu solves this paradox by leveraging AI in areas where they act for the better in terms of neural engagement and ownership of work in students development. Firstly, we have leveraged OpenAI's Whisper to deliver the most sophisticated speech-to-text analysis, which ensures not only accurate transcriptions across diverse accents but fully engages the need for text that unlocks the insights into the learning that educators are missing, as traditional assessment methods are now vulnerable to AI intervention. We have also made usage of an AI-Powered question builder which generates question through analysing educators' learning materials (PDFs, Presentation slides and course content); this automatically generates contextually relevant, pedagogically sound viva questions which accelerates instructor workflow, enabling the furthering of a streamlined process in viva creation.
ai_team_expertise: Our neurodivergent founding team brings a unique combination of lived experience and technical expertise to inclusive assessment technology. Currently, we are  developing LLMs trained on educational discourse patterns, utilising Jex's expertise in data science and statical modelling to create a neurodiversity-informed approach to assessment which engages intelligently with our existing accessibility features. This technical approach works to ensure that our AI systems enhance rather than replace cognitive engagement, creating assessment technology which preserves authentic learning leaning into AI's strength in accessibility and scalability.

GoSquad is solving the problem of overworked educators and disengaged learners.
Today, teachers spend enormous time creating lesson content that is static, text-heavy, and quickly forgotten. This prep burden fuels burnout and drives talented teachers out of the profession, while learners remain disengaged and struggle to retain knowledge.
GoSquad changes this by using AI to turn ordinary curriculum content into cinematic, story-driven learning experiences. Teachers create once, and the content becomes reusable, adaptive, and emotionally engaging. This saves time, reduces workload, and makes learning stick.
We are building the infrastructure that makes learning as captivating as entertainment, while freeing educators to do what only humans can do: inspire.

problem_impact:
The preparation burden on K–12 teachers is unsustainably high. Teachers spend hours every week creating lesson plans, presentations, and worksheets - most of which are static, repetitive, and have limited long-term impact on learners.
This has several negative consequences:
•	Burnout and attrition - Workload is the number one reason teachers leave the profession. In the UK, almost 1 in 3 teachers leave within five years, citing excessive prep time and stress.
•	Lost teaching time - Time spent on content creation reduces time available for high-value, in-person activities like mentoring, guiding discussions, and individual support.
•	Lower engagement and retention - Static materials struggle to hold learners’ attention, which means teachers must work harder to re-engage students, repeating content instead of advancing learning.
By automating and transforming content creation, GoSquad addresses this core pain point - reducing teacher workload while improving learner engagement and outcomes.

solution_explanation:
Problem Recap: Teachers spend countless hours creating lessons that are static, repetitive, and quickly forgotten. This leads to teacher burnout, lost teaching time, and disengaged learners.
How GoSquad solves it:
1.	Content Created Once, Experienced Everywhere: Teachers input their lesson content or curriculum into GoSquad, and our platform transforms it into cinematic, story-driven learning experiences. 4.	Teacher Empowerment and Retention: By reducing prep time and workload, GoSquad allows teachers to dedicate more energy to mentoring, classroom interaction, and personalized support, improving job satisfaction and retention.
5.	Scalable and Adaptive Learning: AI continuously analyzes engagement and performance, helping the platform evolve lessons and optimize learning outcomes over time, something manual approaches cannot achieve at scale.

problem_description:
The Learning Lab is a healthcare education spin-out from Guy’s and St Thomas’ NHS Foundation Trust. We already design and deliver accredited courses, bespoke online programmes, and patient education in partnership with hospitals, charities, and global organisations. This work has reached hundreds of thousands of learners worldwide and won multiple international awards for innovation.

Yet existing Learning Management Systems (LMS) are compliance-driven, generic platforms. They disengage clinicians, waste administrative resource, and fail to build competence. In our NHS survey, only 31% of staff frequently applied learning to practice, while 22% rarely or never did. Meanwhile, the NHS spends £6 billion annually on workforce education (NHS England, 2025).

Our proposed Healthcare LMS (H-LMS) connects role-personalised online modules, AI-driven simulation, and practical competency sign-offs into one seamless ecosystem. It enables clinicians to apply learning directly to practice, managers to track competence in real time, and educators to embed best-practice learning science into design.

The result: a scalable solution to improve efficiency, engagement, and patient safety across healthcare.

Personalised learning: Secure HR data imports automatically generate role- and experience-specific pathways, ensuring training is relevant and practice-focused.


AI-driven simulation: Clinicians apply knowledge in authentic, branching patient scenarios, consulting, testing, diagnosing, and reflecting—making learning active, not passive.


Seamless competency management: Passing learners are auto-scheduled into practical sign-offs. The system handles diary invites, assessor rubrics, and records outcomes in secure e-portfolios, cutting admin burden.


Workforce insight: Real-time dashboards show managers which staff are competent in specific skills, enabling safe redeployment, gap-spotting, and service resilience.


Educator support: An AI design assistant scaffolds courses using learning science, improving quality while reducing design time.

Together, this addresses the core problems: wasted training spend, disengaged clinicians, administrative inefficiency, and slow adoption of new treatments. By shifting from tracking attendance to proving competence, the H-LMS ensures NHS investment translates directly into workforce capability and improved patient safety.

problem_description: DeBiasMe presents a paradigm shift in edtech for AI by addressing the intersection of human cognition and AI systems in research and development. Unlike existing AI literacy tools that focus solely on algorithmic biases, we provide structured learning experiences that teach students to recognise their own cognitive biases (e.g., confirmation bias, anchoring bias, automation bias) and understand how these interact with AI system limitations. Through interactive user-centred learning and real-time reflection supported by our tool for bias recognition & mitigation, we build students' capacity for critical thinking in the AI-integrated future, ensuring that equitable access to AI literacy education across diverse learning communities, particularly those facing barriers in the digital divide.

problem_impact: DeBiasMe tackles the AI literacy crisis in education by developing a comprehensive ecosystem of complementary metacognitive tools that address both human cognitive biases and AI system biases. As students increasingly rely on AI for learning, they develop dangerous dependencies, outsourcing critical thinking and becoming vulnerable to bias amplification cycles. DeBiasMe's comprehensive learning support with tools and workshops transforms passive AI consumers into active collaborators who understand how their thinking patterns interact with AI limitations across both individual learning and collective decision-making contexts. DeBiasMe has demonstrated its potential for responsible AI use across educational contexts, from higher education to middle and high school settings, with applications extending to institutional AIED (AI in Education) governance and policy development.

elevator_pitch: While many companies focus on making AI systems less biased, no one addresses how human cognitive biases interact with AI to undermine critical thinking, especially for students. This is why we created DeBiasMe, an EdTech intervention for bias-aware AI use and AI literacy.
solution_explanation: Our integrated solution introduces "strategic friction" through four interconnected components: 1) Digital Prompt Refinement Tool: detecting cognitive biases before AI interaction 2) Interactive Bias Visualization Map making bias interactions visible in AI responses, 3) Educational Resources: (1) Individual & Collaborative Mapping Tool for reflecting on personal bias patterns and creating collective insights for AIED (AI in Education) governance and policy development, and (2) Evidence-based AI Literacy Workbook/workshop providing structured pause points, scenario-based learning, and practical mitigation strategies. These tools work together: the digital platform provides real-time intervention during AI use, the mapping tool enables classroom-level bias analysis and discussion, while the workbook builds foundational awareness through individual exercises and collaborative action planning frameworks, supported by educational workshops that help teachers integrate these approaches into existing curricula.
program_goals: We want to accelerate DeBiasMe from research prototype to market-ready educational solution through UCL EdTech Labs' evidence-driven methodology. Specifically, we aim to: (1) Strengthen our validation framework: Leverage educational research expertise to develop robust impact metrics that demonstrate learning outcomes and educational effectiveness across diverse student populations. We need systematic evidence collection methods that can convince educators, policymakers, and investors of our approach's efficacy. (2) Scale our AI implementation: Work with AWS architects through UCL's Centre for Digital Innovation to optimise our bias detection algorithms, improve cross-model comparison capabilities, and build enterprise-grade infrastructure that can serve thousands of students simultaneously while maintaining educational effectiveness. (3) Develop sustainable business models: Transform our current academic/informal industry partnerships into revenue-generating relationships while maintaining our commitment to educational equity. We need guidance on pricing strategies that make our tools accessible to underserved communities while ensuring long-term viability. (4) Build strategic partnerships: Connect with educational institutions, policy makers, and EdTech companies that can help us implement bias-aware AI literacy at scale. UCL's global network and connection to UNESCO's AI education guidance positions us perfectly for international expansion. During the programme, we aim to develop:  investor-ready metrics, a scalable technical architecture, and strategic partnerships that enable us to address the AI literacy crisis facing millions of students globally.

ai_problem_solving: Our current prototype utilises GPT-4 via Azure OpenAI for real-time bias detection and hierarchical classification (a working-in-progress system integrates the Claude API). Our system performs bias taxonomy analysis, examining human biases in prompt formulation and AI system biases in responses. We're developing cross-model comparison capabilities enabling users to compare responsible AI use across different LLMs, revealing model-specific bias patterns and empowering end users in AI interactions. This allows us to address the following areas: (1) real-time, scalable bias pattern recognition: Our AI system identifies subtle linguistic markers of confirmation bias, anchoring bias, and framing effects in student prompts as they type, providing immediate feedback that can be used for educational/audit purposes with expert reviews. (2) Cross-model bias analysis at scale: Comparing how different AI models exhibit varying bias patterns across identical prompts requires processing capabilities beyond human capacity. Our system reveals model-specific biases, enabling students to understand that AI systems aren't neutral tools but have distinct bias signatures. (3) Dynamic, personalised metacognitive scaffolding: Traditional bias education relies on static examples and retrospective analysis. Our AI creates personalised bias visualisations that adapt to each student's specific interaction patterns, generating custom examples and mitigation strategies based on their actual AI usage behaviour. This level of individualised, context-aware intervention is impossible through conventional educational methods.  With AI, we transform abstract bias concepts and implications into experiential and personalised learning when students need them most - during actual AI interactions where bias amplification happens.

This combination of academic rigour, practical AI implementation experience, industry partnerships, and direct engagement with student populations positions our team to develop evidence-based solutions that bridge the gap between AI technical capabilities and educational impact.

elevator_pitch: Somarity turns any curriculum into personalized AI tutoring. Students get tailored practice, visual explanations, and perfect-timing guidance to build confidence. Teachers save hours with auto-generated materials plus clear insights on who needs help most.
solution_explanation:
1. Teacher starts with their plan. They enter a learning objective or upload the week's scheme of work.

2. Auto lesson draft. Somarity generates a ready lesson, local examples, and short exercises that the teacher can edit in minutes based on the school's curriculum/exam board.

3. Student assistant diagnoses. When a student works, the assistant finds the step where the student got stuck and gives a targeted next step, hints, worked solutions, or a quick explanation in words, symbols or a short diagram.

4. Adaptive practice & spaced review. The system adjusts difficulty and brings back fragile topics at the right time so learning sticks.

5. Teacher dashboard. Teachers can share learning resources, assignments and practice exams to classrooms through system generated share codes, see who is stuck and on which concept, making small-group support practical even in large classes.

6. Student Practice Lab. AI generates varied practice questions tailored to specific curriculum and exam boards. Students get multiple versions of challenging problems with different contexts, learning to apply concepts rather than memorise specific questions.

7. Visual learning support. AI creates animations and infographics that break down complex STEM concepts into digestible visual explanations, helping students truly understand rather than just remember.

research_evidence:
Teacher Capacity and Support: Educators in low resource settings themselves often face challenges. Many lack mastery of the content they teach (SDI 2010–2014, cited in WBRO, 2022), struggle with recruitment and deployment issues, especially in remote areas, and lack adequate incentives or resources for professional development. This impacts the quality and consistency of instruction.

Need for differentiated Instruction: Traditional classroom models often fail to cater to diverse student learning levels. Research strongly indicates the effectiveness of pedagogical interventions that tailor teaching to individual student needs, whether teacher-led or facilitated by technology (Evans & Popova, 2016). However, implementing such personalized approaches ("Teaching at the Right Level") is often resource-intensive and challenging in contexts with high pupil-teacher ratios. While EdTech presents potential solutions, its effectiveness hinges on addressing specific contextual challenges, such as leveraging technology for appropriately-leveled instruction and overcoming input shortages (Rodriguez-Segura, 2022). Interventions focused on self-led learning reinforcement and improving instruction quality show particular promise (Rodriguez-Segura, 2022).

References
David K. Evans, Anna Popova, What Really Works to Improve Learning in Developing Countries? An Analysis of Divergent Findings in Systematic Reviews, The World Bank Research Observer, Volume 31, Issue 2, August 2016, Pages 242–270, https://doi.org/10.1093/wbro/lkw004

Daniel Rodriguez-Segura, EdTech in Developing Countries: A Review of the Evidence, The World Bank Research Observer, Volume 37, Issue 2, August 2022, Pages 171–203, https://doi.org/10.1093/wbro/lkab011

https://openknowledge.worldbank.org/bitstreams/6ac08b1a-d072-4727-8678-d42bbee86a8a/download
ai_problem_solving:
1) Scale of personalised practice: A single teacher can’t create many high-quality, varied questions or spot each student’s micro-errors. AI can generate many different practice scenarios and find the exact step where a student struggles for every learner, in real time.

2) Speed and cost for engaging rich content: Creating local, curriculum-aligned lesson materials and varied question banks by hand is slow and expensive. AI does it fast and cheaply. 

3)AI also turns material into engaging animations and infographics for students to actually understand hard STEM ideas

4) Better practice than past papers: AI can make many versions of hard problems with different contexts so students learn to apply ideas, not memorise specific questions.

problem_description:
Medical students and junior doctors face enormous pressure preparing for high-stakes board and licensing exams. Existing study resources are often:
- Expensive – traditional review courses and textbooks are priced out of reach for many students.
- Fragmented – content is scattered across multiple platforms, forcing students to juggle different tools.
- Outdated or generic – many question banks do not reflect the latest exam formats, guidelines, or region-specific curricula.
- Unpersonalized – students receive little feedback on their weak areas, making it hard to focus their revision.
- Inaccessible – especially in emerging markets, there is limited access to affordable, high-quality exam preparation resources.

As a result, students waste valuable time, money, and energy searching for the right study materials, while still feeling underprepared for their exams.

problem_impact:
Financial Pressure – Many are forced to spend on traditional review courses, textbooks, which are unaffordable for a large portion of students, particularly in lower- and middle-income regions.

Inefficient Learning – Without access to integrated platforms, students waste time switching between scattered resources that may be outdated or misaligned with the actual exam curriculum. This reduces study efficiency and leaves knowledge gaps.

Increased Stress and Burnout – Preparing for high-stakes exams while managing clinical duties is already overwhelming. The absence of personalized feedback or clear progress tracking amplifies stress and uncertainty.

Geographic Inequities – Students in certain regions lack access to localized or affordable preparation materials, putting them at a disadvantage compared to peers in more resource-rich settings.

Career Setbacks – Poor preparation leads to higher failure rates in board and licensing exams. This delays career progression, increases costs (re-sitting exams, extending training), and in some cases results in doctors leaving the profession.

problem_description:
Schools are under growing pressure to engage every learner, assess more inclusively, and reduce teacher workload. Yet classroom learning still relies on one dominant format: writing.

This creates a barrier for millions of children. Those with dyslexia, ADHD, speech and language needs, or English as an additional language often have the ideas and knowledge but struggle to express them on paper. Their understanding goes unnoticed, their confidence erodes, and their potential remains hidden.

The scale of the issue is clear:
- Only 61% of primary pupils in England met expected standards in reading, writing and maths in 2024 (DfE).
- 34% of primary pupils struggle with handwriting, and up to 73% lack writing proficiency by secondary (DfE/NAEP).
- Despite oracy being part of the curriculum, less than half of teachers feel confident delivering it (APPG on Oracy).

Teachers are actively asking for tools that:
- Engage students who struggle with traditional methods.
- Allow them to evidence understanding beyond written tasks.
- Save time and workload while supporting diverse learners.

At its core, the problem is that verbal communication is misunderstood. It is treated as something children will naturally pick up, rather than a skill that needs structured practice just like reading or maths.

problem_impact:
For children, the reliance on writing as the main way to show learning creates daily frustration and missed opportunities. Many have rich ideas and strong verbal skills, but these remain invisible when everything depends on handwriting or spelling. This leads to lower attainment, loss of confidence, and disengagement from school. Pupils with dyslexia, ADHD, or speech and language needs are disproportionately affected, which widens attainment gaps and harms long-term social mobility.

For teachers, the impact is twofold. First, they struggle to capture a true picture of what children know because so much evidence is tied to written tasks. This limits their ability to plan effectively and to celebrate progress. Second, it adds workload pressure. Marking written work is time-consuming, and there are few scalable tools for assessing verbal communication. Teachers want to support oracy but often lack the time, training, or resources to do it consistently.

The result is a cycle where children feel unheard and teachers feel unsupported, with both groups missing out on the benefits of structured speaking and listening practice.

elevator_pitch: Telmi is the podcast creation app that helps children learn by speaking. We turn verbal communication into a structured, gamified skill, giving every child the chance to build confidence, creativity, and critical thinking.
solution_explanation:
Telmi makes speaking and listening a daily classroom habit by giving children structured opportunities to practise purposeful communication through podcasting. Instead of relying on writing as the only way to show understanding, children record short, guided episodes in response to curriculum-linked prompts. This allows them to express ideas aloud, build fluency, and demonstrate knowledge in ways that are more accessible and confidence-boosting.

For teachers, Telmi reduces workload by providing ready-made, age-appropriate prompts that can be used instantly or adapted to fit lesson objectives. AI transcription and reflection tools make it easy to review recordings without listening to every one, so teachers can quickly assess understanding and give feedback. The platform also allows children to work independently or in pairs, so teachers can run activities without needing to orchestrate whole-class speaking sessions.

By focusing on voice rather than video, Telmi removes the pressure of appearance and makes oracy practice inclusive for shy children, neurodivergent learners, and those with additional needs. Early trials show that 75% of children feel more confident speaking after just one use, and teachers report uncovering skills that were previously hidden by written tasks.

In short, Telmi bridges the gap between the importance of oracy and the lack of scalable tools to deliver it. It empowers children to find their voice and gives teachers a simple, evidence-based way to embed oracy in everyday learning while saving time.
program_goals:
By joining UCL EdTech Labs, we want to take Telmi from a promising early-stage product to a rigorously validated, scalable solution for schools. Specifically, we aim to:

Strengthen the evidence base: Partner with UCL researchers to measure Telmi’s impact on confidence, comprehension, and inclusion, particularly for neurodivergent learners.

1. Refine our AI responsibly: Work with UCL’s academic and technical experts to develop our teacher-trained AI engine using Amazon Bedrock and Transcribe, ensuring safety, transparency, and pedagogical quality.

2. Build for scale: Leverage AWS guidance to stress-test our architecture for classroom-level rollout, so schools can adopt Telmi confidently and securely.

3. Expand our network: Connect with the wider UCL ecosystem of educators, technologists, and partners to accelerate adoption in both the UK and international markets.

Our ultimate goal is to make oracy as teachable and trackable as literacy, giving every child the chance to be heard  and this programme offers the research rigour, technical expertise, and ecosystem we need to achieve that.

ai_problem_solving:
AI enables Telmi to make structured speaking practice scalable in a way that traditional methods cannot. Teachers know oracy matters, but without support it is difficult to embed it daily and to track progress across a whole class.

AI helps us solve three problems that could not be addressed otherwise:

1. Generating high-quality prompts at scale
Creating curriculum-linked, age-appropriate speaking prompts takes significant teacher time. With Amazon Bedrock, we generate scaffolded prompts and reflection questions that adapt to different subjects and ability levels. This saves teachers hours of preparation while ensuring relevance.

2. Making verbal expression visible
Through services like Amazon Transcribe and Comprehend, spoken podcasts can be automatically transcribed and analysed. This allows teachers to quickly see what a child has said without listening to every recording, and gives children the chance to see their spoken ideas in writing.

3. Building a teacher-trained feedback loop
Our AI improves based on how teachers edit or adapt prompts, not on children’s data. This human-in-the-loop approach ensures the system learns from pedagogy, not from sensitive student voices. Without AI, it would be impossible to create this scalable cycle of teacher expertise shaping future prompts.

In short, AI allows Telmi to turn oracy from an aspirational skill into a daily, measurable habit in real classrooms, while protecting privacy and reducing teacher workload.

elevator_pitch: A pioneering closed-loop platform powered by an agentic system for integrated academic and career pathways, enabling efficient transformation for the education sector.
solution_explanation:
Apply U is building an agentic system dedicated to educational decision-making. Distinct agents—handling program matching, labor-market forecasting, and policy compliance—work cooperatively. An embedded debiasing protocol actively ensures fairness, while a multi-lingual layer supports over fifty languages. Moreover, our pioneering 6-dimensional explainable matching model integrates academic, disciplines, skills, project experience, work history, and career objectives to deliver optimal program recommendations and significantly boost algorithmic trustworthiness.

When benchmarked against prevailing alternatives, the system demonstrates a step-change in both accuracy and operational efficiency. Traditional study-abroad agencies, which rely on manual counsellor workflows, typically require weeks to complete a single applicant cycle at costs thousand dollars per client. Rule-based recommender engines accelerate turnaround to days but plateau at 70% accuracy and cannot accommodate rapid data shifts. General LLM deliver real-time responses, yet their domain-agnostic tuning yields hallucination and offers scant control over fairness. In contrast, our agentic system provides domain-specific guidance under 5 minutes, sustains 95% match accuracy in real world validation. Crucially, our agentic system is algorithmic rather than manual, the architecture exhibits notable scalability in geographical, linguistic, and disciplinary terms, thereby accommodating regional nuances and shifting labor market conditions alongside university admission criteria.

In conclusion, Apply U offers the first AI educational decision-making agentic system to combine real-time, agentic reasoning; cross-lingual, bias-mitigated personalization; and efficient service delivery at human-level accuracy. By collapsing what was once a labor-bound, weeks-long, and costly process into an agentic workflow, the platform redefines the approach for academic and career guidance and global talent acquisition.
program_goals:
We aim to refine our agentic AI system through expert mentorship, ensuring it delivers maximum value to all stakeholders.

By joining this programme, we hope to build global partnerships and scale Apply U’s impact, democratizing access to world-class education and career opportunities worldwide.
ai_problem_solving:
Personalization at Massive Scale
Every student has a unique profile spanning academic records, skills, work experience, and career aspirations. AI enables real-time, multi-dimensional matching across millions of programs worldwide, sustaining 90% accuracy. Human counsellors or static filters simply cannot process this data volume at speed.

Dynamic Adaptation to Changing Data
Admission criteria, visa policies, and labor market trends shift constantly. AI agents can continuously learn from new data, updating their knowledge within minutes, whereas traditional systems require manual reprogramming and are often outdated by the time students use them.

Bias Mitigation and Fair Access
Human advisors can introduce unconscious bias. Apply U’s embedded debiasing protocol systematically checks recommendations for fairness across gender, region, and socioeconomic background which is something infeasible to do manually at scale.

Explainability and Trust
Our 6-dimensional explainable matching model shows students why a program is recommended (academic fit, skills, projects, work history, career goals). This level of transparency builds trust and confidence in decision-making, which a black-box agent process or generic chatbot cannot offer.

End-to-End Integration
We have a cooperative agentic system, linking program prediction, labor-market forecasting, and compliance checking into a single workflow. This collapses what used to be a multi-week process into under 5 minutes while outperforming human-level accuracy.

elevator_pitch: ThinkiAI is the AI-native tutor for kids aged 7–11. It adapts to each child’s learning style, lets parents guide teaching methods, and motivates progress through daily tasks, activities and a AI powered notebook that build on each other for lasting mastery.
solution_explanation:
How ThinkiAI Solves the Problem

Affordable tutoring at scale
Private tutors are expensive and inaccessible to many families. By using AI, ThinkiAI delivers a personalized tutor at a fraction of the cost of human tutoring, making high-quality support available to every child.

Safe, voice-native learning
The app is voice-first, enabling natural conversation. This allows the AI to pick up on the child’s thought process and adapt its teaching method in real time, while parents can set the educational approach that best suits their values.

Curriculum-aligned daily tasks
ThinkiAI ensures learning stays on track with national curriculum standards. Tasks build on each other and are never solved for the child. Instead, the AI gives hints that encourage critical thinking and cognitive growth. An ELO system continuously adjusts difficulty to the child’s level.

Motivation through rewards
To unlock time on activities, children must complete their daily tasks. Parents can choose between two reward options:
- AI-generated activities (e.g. experiments, stories) that spark curiosity and creativity.
- Device screen-time control, where access is unlocked only after tasks are completed.

Why this matters
Children often struggle to see the long-term benefits of education, unlike adults. By tying progress to immediate rewards and fun exploration, ThinkiAI bridges that gap, motivating consistent learning, supporting cognitive development, and empowering parents with a safe, affordable alternative to traditional tutoring.

elevator_pitch: `EdCodeD` is an AI platform that tackles K-12 academic debt. We create hyper-personalized, gamified learning paths based on a student's unique cognitive profile, empowering every child to learn and succeed.
solution_explanation:
edcoded is designed to solve the problem of K-12 academic debt by leveraging AI to accomplish tasks that are impossible for humans to perform at scale. Our solution is a holistic, multi-faceted platform that works as follows:

1. AI-Powered Cognitive Assessment: Pinpointing the Root Cause
Unlike a traditional, standardized test that only measures a student's performance on a given subject, EdCodeD's AI performs a deep, diagnostic analysis of a student's cognitive and developmental capacities. This couldn't be done otherwise because a human teacher lacks the capacity to analyze the vast amount of data points needed for a comprehensive, real-time assessment for every student.

Our platform uses machine learning models and natural language processing to analyze:

Response time and patterns: Is the student struggling with working memory, or do they have difficulty with spatial reasoning?

Behavioral data: Is the student disengaging from certain types of problems? Are they demonstrating frustration?

Error analysis: The AI doesn't just note a wrong answer; it analyzes the type of error to identify the underlying misconception.

This process generates a detailed "cognitive profile" for each student, revealing the root causes of their academic debt, such as weaknesses in executive function, attention, or information processing.

2. Hyper-Personalized, Gamified Pedagogical Strategies
Based on the AI-generated cognitive profile, EdCodeD's AI then dynamically creates a hyper-personalized, gamified learning path. This is a crucial step that is impossible for a human educator to replicate. A teacher can differentiate instruction for small groups, but they cannot create a unique, engaging lesson for every student in their class, adapting the content and method on a moment-to-moment basis.

Our platform addresses the problem in the following ways:

Targeted Cognitive Training: If a student's profile shows a weakness in working memory, the platform won't just repeat the lesson; it will introduce a series of mini-games and challenges designed specifically to strengthen that cognitive skill.

Gamification for Engagement: The use of game mechanics like points, badges, and leaderboards helps to maintain student motivation and engagement. This is particularly vital for neurodivergent students, as it makes the learning process less intimidating and more aligned with their interests.

Real-time Adaptation: As the student progresses, the AI continuously adjusts the difficulty and style of the learning path to maintain an optimal balance between challenge and success, preventing both boredom and frustration.

3. A Unified Platform for All Stakeholders
The platform unifies the student, parent, and educator experience, providing a complete ecosystem to address the problem.

For Students: They receive a learning experience that is uniquely tailored to them, helping them close academic gaps in a fun and engaging way.

For Parents: They gain access to a clear dashboard with real-time insights into their child's progress, strengths, and weaknesses. This empowers them to provide targeted support at home, which they previously couldn't do.

For Educators: Teachers are freed from the burden of large-scale administrative and diagnostic tasks. The EdCodeD dashboard provides them with actionable insights, allowing them to focus their time and energy on meaningful, human-centered teaching and mentorship.
program_goals:
Participating in the UCL AI Ed Tech Fellowship is a critical step in turning EdCodeD from a promising idea into a validated, ethical, and scalable solution. My primary goals are:

To ground our product in rigorous research: The fellowship's emphasis on evidence-based development and the UCL Knowledge Lab's expertise in AI and education will be invaluable. I want to use their proven research methodologies to rigorously validate that our cognitive assessment is accurate and that our gamified pedagogical strategies effectively close academic debt, especially for neurodivergent students.

To build a framework for ethical and responsible AI: UCL's focus on "AI for People and Planet" and addressing issues of bias and data privacy aligns perfectly with my mission. I want to develop a robust framework for EdCodeD that ensures our AI is equitable, transparent, and protects student data, setting a new standard for responsible EdTech.

To leverage the network for strategic growth: The fellowship provides access to a powerful ecosystem of world-leading researchers, mentors, investors, and policymakers. I aim to use this network to refine our business model, secure our pilot programs, and develop a strategic roadmap for wider, more impactful adoption in schools.
ai_problem_solving:
AI plays a critical role in solving problems that are impossible for humans to manage at scale. For EdCodeD, AI is essential for two key functions that couldn't be solved otherwise:

Precise, Real-time Cognitive and Developmental Assessment: A human educator in a classroom of 30 or more students simply cannot perform a detailed cognitive assessment for every child in real-time. It is a monumental task that requires a level of data collection and analysis far beyond human capacity. AI, through machine learning and natural language processing, can analyze a student's responses, time spent on tasks, and behavioral patterns to build a unique cognitive profile for each learner. This allows EdCodeD to pinpoint the exact root causes of academic debt, a level of diagnostic precision that is not possible with traditional assessments.

Hyper-Personalized Pedagogical Strategy Generation: Creating a customized, gamified learning path for every single student is a logistical impossibility for a human teacher. While a teacher can provide a differentiated lesson for a small group, they cannot create and deliver a truly unique pedagogical strategy for each individual student, adapting to their strengths and weaknesses on a moment-to-moment basis. AI solves this by dynamically generating content, adjusting the difficulty of games and lessons, and providing real-time feedback based on the student's unique profile. This ensures that every student, especially those who are neurodivergent, receives the exact support they need to close their learning gaps in a way that is engaging and effective.

solution_explanation:
MSL addresses the dual challenge of disengaged students and overextended teachers by creating an adaptive ecosystem for science education. For students, our AI-driven algorithms analyse interaction patterns (question attempts, time on task, misconceptions) and tailor lesson pathways in real time; and provide personalised reinforcement or enrichment to keep learners engaged and progressing.

For teachers, the platform consolidates these signals into intuitive dashboards that highlight at-risk students, equity gaps, and comprehension trends. Instead of relying on delayed test results or anecdotal observations, teachers gain a live feed of actionable insights that guide timely interventions.

At the school level, MSL’s aggregated data helps leaders track pilot outcomes, equity metrics, and curriculum alignment, supporting both accountability and funding conversations. Our solution therefore solves the student learning gap at its root while simultaneously giving educators the tools and visibility they need to manage classrooms more effectively; something traditional LMS platforms cannot deliver.

problem_description: Current science curriculum in the UK is outdated and hyper-focused on teaching students facts instead of imparting the critical thinking skills, creativity, and collaborative spirit that they need in order to succeed in life and/or become the next scientific leaders and entrepreneurs. Students are taught science primarily through cognitivism and behaviorism and are then penalized for not being able to connect these abstract facts back to their realities when it is the school's role to make their learning meaningful to their lives. This type of pedagogy leads to early disengagement in science classrooms, which is particularly upsetting since every child is born a scientist and this way of learning science is not what this field represents. My aim is to narrow the divide between scientific theory and reality by creating a product that utilizes a pedagogical format that will foster a child's natural curiosity and will teach scientific concepts through the scientific method and its practices.

problem_impact: Every child is born a scientist - meaning that every child is born as a curious individual who learns about the world around them through observation, exploration, and experimentation. When you take such an individual and place them into a classroom where statistics show that more than 35% of them will fail to understand the subject at a meaningful level, you start to question how modern scientific pedagogy has failed our children. As a secondary school teacher, I have watched the power of an inquiry-led curriculum - it allows teachers to teach students the soft skills that they need to succeed in life while students get to do what they are born to do: learn through exploration and connections. Taking that curiosity away by teaching students science through facts and not its principles leads to disengagement and takes away the power that students otherwise may have in their learning experience. This is particularly harmful to students who already come from disenfranchised backgrounds.
customer_type: {Other,"Teachers (K - 12)"}
elevator_pitch: Current EdTech products are catered towards helping students learn science content to pass their exams - this type of pedagogy is not true to the scientific practices or science's history of entrepreneurship. My product will help raise up the next scientific leaders.
solution_explanation:
This learning platform will utilize generative AI tools to help students build research projects and
master difficult scientific concepts and practices through personal inquiry. The platform will first
build a rough cognitive profile for each student by utilizing teachers' notes and previous years'
test scores across all subjects. Based on this profile, the platform would present content
according to the student's specific learning needs and/or preferred learning methods, and create a
baseline scaffold that would be used to teach and guide students through initial lessons but will
continuously evolve to become more robust and individualized as the student interacts with the
AI. Once the cognitive profile has been built, the student would be able to interact with the
platform to talk about their curiosities and dreams for the future. The system and student will
build several different inquiries/projects that the student wants to pursue, with the teacher and
system scaffolding the observation and researching phase of the scientific method. After the
student has made their choice of inquiries, the platform would organize the required curriculum
into said inquiries so that as the student continues to do background research, build hypotheses,
and design their experiment, the platform would help teach the curriculum-required scientific
concepts that underlie their inquiry. Utilizing the cognitive profile and recommended scaffolds,
the system will utilize videos, diagrams, readings, and questions to teach these concepts.
Students will eventually reach the testing phase of their experiment or product. Not all
experiments might be tested outside of the digital world, but teachers can help students choose
their favorite projects and present those findings in science fairs, or help them create their chosen
product outside of the digital platform. In a typical 1-hour lesson, students will be learning
scientific concepts and practices for about 30 minutes. In the next 30 minutes, teachers will
develop important soft skills in scientific communication through facilitation of discussions
about projects and the underlying scientific concepts, encourage collaboration between different
projects, and challenge students' critical thinking skills. Teachers will also always have access to
student work in the system and be able to control the degree of control that AI will have in the
scaffolding and use of materials. For example, if teachers want to have everyone learning the
exact same material at the same time, then they can choose to let the AI only provide
differentiated learning tools to students. However, it would be recommended that teachers allow
students to have more choice in their learning since this would increase engagement and
encourage the critical thinking skills that are missing in today's classrooms.
program_goals: I hope to gain the skills and resources necessary to make this product a reality. Then, I hope this product can fundamentally transform how students learn science and raise the next generation of scientific entrepreneurs and researchers from all types of diverse backgrounds.
ai_problem_solving: AI would provide the one-on-one help that students need to learn through personal inquiry while also helping create the cognitive profile as each student, from which a scaffolded curriculum can be built.

problem_description:
First-time managers are expected to lead, coach and communicate effectively, yet most have never been trained to do so and organisations have no clear way to identify or close those skill gaps. 82% of managers who enter management positions have not had any formal management and leadership training; they are "accidental managers" (CMI, 2023).

Traditional corporate training and HR systems measure attendance, not capability. L&D teams rely on subjective feedback or annual reviews to assess communication, empathy or decision-making - making it impossible to know who’s truly ready to manage people or handle high-stakes conversations.

This creates a measurable business cost: poor managerial communication drives disengagement, attrition and lost productivity, costing employers billions each year. Poor management in the US is costing ~$960 billion to $1.2 trillion per year. Globally, the cost of poor management approaches $7 trillion, or ~10% of the world's GDP (Gallup, 2024).

Lanura solves this by combining AI-driven skills diagnostics (to pinpoint behavioural gaps) with adaptive voice simulations (to practise real workplace conversations). Together, they enable organisations to:
- Detect skill gaps early and help managers be more self-aware
- Give managers a safe space to practise and receive personalised coaching
- Track measurable behaviour change over time, and tie it to business performance metrics

The biggest pain is felt by L&D and HR leaders in mid-sized organisations - those under pressure to prove ROI on training but lacking data or scalable tools to build human skills effectively. 

Although we’re starting with first-time managers as our initial wedge - where the pain is most acute and measurable - our long-term vision is to expand Lanura across the wider workforce and eventually into schools and universities to build human skills earlier in the learning journey.

elevator_pitch: Lanura helps first-time managers master the conversations that shape team performance. We combine skills diagnostics with voice simulations that deliver personalised coaching. Unlike traditional training, Lanura makes skill development continuous and embedded in the flow of work.
solution_explanation:
Most first-time managers are promoted without training and struggle to lead difficult conversations, costing organisations billions in disengagement and turnover. Lanura bridges this gap by combining skills intelligence with practice-based learning.
1) Diagnose: Our AI diagnostic identifies communication and leadership skill gaps by analysing behavioural signals, tone, and conversational patterns.
2) Practise: Managers engage in adaptive, voice-based simulations that mirror real workplace challenges, such as performance reviews or feedback sessions, with the AI responding contextually in real time.
3) Measure & Reinforce: Personalised feedback, nudges and analytics help managers build confidence, track improvement and connect progress to business metrics like engagement and retention.

This creates a closed learning loop — from diagnosis → practice → measurement → improvement that transforms soft-skill training from theoretical to actionable, measurable and scalable.

- Refine our business model and impact narrative through expert mentorship and the Labs’ world-leading guidance on business modelling, customer validation and sustainable funding.

By the end of the programme, we want to emerge with a research-validated MVP, a clear go-to-market strategy and a compelling evidence-backed story that positions Lanura as a global leader in skills intelligence, in-flow learning.

problem_description:
The core problem is the conflict between the rapid adoption of GenAI in higher education and the urgent need for academic integrity and evidenced pedagogical efficacy.

Currently:

Academic Risk & Integrity: The majority of students (88%) are already using general-purpose AI tools like ChatGPT, which are inherently unreliable and prone to the "Hallucination Trap" (Deck Page 2). This creates an immediate, unmanageable risk of academic misconduct and misinformation, undermining the value of the education institutions provide. Institutions have no "Safe AI" option.

Unmet Learning Needs: The failure of current support systems to scale, combined with the complexities of university-level material, leads to a significant Education Gap. This is especially acute for non-native English speakers due to the "Language Gap" and for any student facing a "Shyness Barrier" that prevents them from seeking help, directly impacting retention and outcomes.

In summary, universities are forced to choose between embracing unverified, high-risk AI or providing unscalable, expensive human support. There is no scalable, evidence-led solution designed specifically to uphold academic standards while radically improving learner outcomes.

problem_impact:
The key groups negatively impacted by the problem are Students (the end-users) and Universities/Institutions (the customers and gatekeepers of academic standards).

Here is how the identified problem—the lack of a safe, academically verified, and scalable AI learning companion—has negatively impacted these groups:

1. Negative Impact on Students

The existing environment prevents a significant portion of the student body from achieving their potential, leading to disengagement and poor outcomes:

Academic Failure Risk (The Hallucination Trap): When students use open AI tools (which 88% already do ), they face a high risk of obtaining unverified, fake information. This directly compromises the quality of their coursework, increases the chance of academic misconduct, and undermines their learning.

Knowledge Gaps (The Shyness Barrier): The fear of judgment often prevents students from asking "basic" questions. This leaves core knowledge gaps unaddressed, building a weak foundation for more complex topics.

Inaccessibility (The Language Gap & Cognitive Load):
             -International students are alienated by complex jargon, making course material unnecessarily difficult to absorb.

             -The high Cognitive Load of complex topics, without simplified explanations, causes the information to fail to stick. This creates an Education Gap.

2. Negative Impact on Universities and Corporate Institutions

Institutions face operational, financial, and reputational risks due to the failure of current solutions:

  -Integrity and Liability Risk: Institutions face a significant challenge managing students who are actively using unverified AI. This creates a liability risk by introducing misinformation into high-stakes learning and training environments (e.g., the safety protocols mentioned in the US Pharma Institution example).

  -Unscalable Support Model: Traditional human tutors cannot provide 24/7 availability. This inability to scale means that institutions cannot meet the rising student demand for personalized, instant academic support without incurring prohibitive costs.

  -Poor Outcomes and Retention: The combined effects of the Hallucination Trap, Cognitive Load, and Shyness Barrier reduce overall student engagement and academic success, negatively impacting institutional retention rates and reputation (implied by the focus on the Education Gap ).

ai_problem_solving:
Specifically, AI is the only technology that can solve the following combination of problems:

1. Guaranteed Factual Accuracy at Scale

    The Unsolvable Gap: A human tutor provides high accuracy but is limited in availability and is not scalable. General AI (like ChatGPT) is available 24/7, but risks the "Hallucination Trap" with unverified information.

The AI Solution: Alcadura uses AI to index and process material strictly from verified courseware. This allows it to guarantee 100% Factual Accuracy Verified against courseware while operating with 24/7 Availability. This hybrid of absolute accuracy and endless scale cannot be achieved by any other method.

2. Instant, Hyper-Personalized Content Generation

    The Unsolvable Gap: A human tutor takes time to create quizzes, simplify concepts, and understand cultural context for every student. This leads to a lack of scalability (high cost, slow delivery).

    The AI Solution: Alcadura's AI engine instantly transforms static course material into a personalized learning experience by:

        Simplification: Offering concepts using the "Explain like I'm 12" feature.

Gamification: Auto-Generating Quizzes and memory tools on demand from the ingested PDFs and lectures.

Inclusivity: Providing Culturally aware and personalized support that adapts to individual learning styles, addressing the "Language Gap" and "Shyness Barrier".

AI is the only way to deliver this level of instant, customized content creation to hundreds of thousands of students without a corresponding explosion in labor costs.

3. Eliminating the Shyness and Cognitive Barriers

    The Unsolvable Gap: A student suffering from the "Shyness Barrier" will never ask critical questions to a human tutor for fear of judgment.

The AI Solution: The AI Buddy provides a non-judgmental, pressure-free environment where students can ask any question, no matter how basic, thereby overcoming a deep-seated psychological barrier to learning that human interaction often fails to solve. This directly lowers the Cognitive Load and begins to close the Education Gap.

elevator_pitch: Drimmly is an AI career companion for students and parents that turns uncertainty into a clear, personalised study and career pathway. Continuously adapting to a student’s plan based on their goals, strengths, and real progress over time.
solution_explanation:
Drimmly solves the guidance gap by replacing one-off, generic career advice with continuous, personalised decision support that students can actually follow. The product starts by capturing a student’s context through short assessments and conversation: interests, strengths, constraints, aspirations, and what “success” means to them. (In practice, many students can describe what they don’t want, but struggle to articulate a direction.)

From there, Drimmly generates a pathway made of simple building blocks: possible career matches, the skills that matter, recommended subjects/modules, and a sequence of achievable milestones. Each milestone is paired with concrete actions (projects, learning resources, extracurriculars, internships, applications) so the student always has an obvious next step, not just a vague recommendation.

Crucially, the plan is not static. Drimmly tracks what the student actually does and how they respond over time, then updates the pathway accordingly: it identifies missing prerequisites, detects when motivation drops, flags unrealistic timelines, and offers alternative routes that still lead to the same goal. Parents get a clear, supportive view of progress and priorities, which reduces conflict and guesswork. (It often shifts the home conversation from “What are you going to do with your life?” to “What’s the next step this week?”)

For schools, Drimmly provides scalable, consistent guidance that complements counsellors rather than replacing them, while keeping safety and privacy central—especially for minors—through age-based guardrails and controlled data retention.
program_goals: We want to validate Drimmly’s impact with real students and parents in the UK, refine our product with expert feedback, and prove measurable outcomes like improved clarity, commitment to a pathway, and sustained progress over time. (We’re building for a moment in a student’s life that feels heavy, so we want to get it right.) We also want to strengthen our go-to-market with schools and partners, sharpen our evidence and evaluation approach, and leave the programme with a pilot-ready, scalable model and a strong network of mentors and educators to accelerate adoption.

ai_problem_solving: AI lets us deliver continuous, personalised guidance at scale by turning messy, fast-changing career information and student signals into a living plan that updates over time, which isn’t feasible with human counsellors or static assessments alone. Our AI can maintain long-term context about a student, detect gaps and inconsistencies in their goals and evidence, and translate this into clear next steps, resources, and progress tracking without requiring hours of 1:1 support. It also enables consistent quality, availability, and affordability for every student and parent, instead of guidance being limited by school capacity, geography, or budget.

We have experience building LLM-based coaching and tutoring flows, retrieval systems over trusted sources, and privacy-by-design infrastructure suitable for minors, including age-based safety controls and secure data handling.

co_founders:
Johanna Wang – Founder & CEO, former management consultant at KPMG and Accenture, specialising in career strategy, positioning, and go-to-market. Built JD Mastery and supported 2,000+ international students, leading product, methodology, and distribution.
Daniel Pelnar – Technical Co-founder, background in Computer Science (Imperial College London), with experience in software development and AI integration. Responsible for platform development and building AI-assisted workflows.
problem_description:
International students in the UK face a structurally inefficient hiring process, where large volumes of applications result in very low response rates and limited access to decision-makers.

Most candidates rely on online applications, but hiring systems are increasingly automated and filter CVs based on keywords rather than real capability. As a result, many applicants optimise for passing systems rather than demonstrating meaningful value.

This creates a clear mismatch: capable candidates struggle to be identified, while employers receive high volumes of low-signal applications.

From my experience supporting over 2,000 students, 80–90% receive no interview invitations despite months of applying, highlighting a systemic inefficiency rather than an individual skills gap.
problem_causes: Lack of Training/Expertise, Lack of Resources (Human): Professional context, Lack of Resources (Time), Lack of a technology, Lack of awareness, Lack of Social Network: Community, Family, Peer Support

problem_impact:
International students and early-career professionals experience prolonged job search cycles with little to no feedback, often applying for months without receiving interview opportunities.

This leads to significant negative impact across three areas:
	•	Loss of confidence and motivation, as repeated rejection or silence creates uncertainty about their own ability
	•	Inefficient use of time and resources, with candidates spending months applying to roles that are unlikely to convert
	•	Limited access to career progression, particularly for international students facing visa constraints, where delayed employment directly affects their ability to remain in the UK

In many cases, highly capable candidates are filtered out before reaching any human interaction, meaning their actual potential is never assessed.

As a result, individuals are not only delayed in entering the workforce but may also accept suboptimal roles or exit the market entirely.

elevator_pitch: JD Mastery is an AI-enabled career learning platform that helps international students navigate job markets through structured, decision-based workflows rather than traditional application processes.
solution_explanation:
JD Mastery addresses inefficiencies in the hiring process by reframing job search as a structured learning journey rather than a trial-and-error application process.

The platform teaches users how to make effective career decisions through a step-by-step workflow that mirrors real-world job search behaviour.

Users are guided through four core stages:
	•	defining direction and positioning based on strengths and market needs
	•	building high-signal professional assets that communicate value
	•	identifying and accessing opportunities through targeted research and outreach
	•	developing communication skills for interviews and professional conversations

The key innovation lies in transforming implicit, experience-based knowledge into an explicit, teachable system.

AI enables this system to scale by providing personalised guidance at each stage. Based on user input, the platform generates structured prompts, suggests next actions, and delivers feedback on positioning and communication.

This ensures that users are not only consuming content, but actively learning through guided decision-making and iteration.

As a result, users develop a repeatable skillset for navigating job markets independently, rather than relying on one-off coaching or static resources.
program_goals:
Through this programme, my primary goal is to further develop JD Mastery as a scalable, AI-enabled learning platform.

While the current model has validated strong demand through structured programmes and early revenue, the next step is to translate the methodology into a productised system that can scale beyond direct delivery.

Specifically, I aim to:
	•	refine the AI-assisted user workflow and learning experience
	•	test how personalised guidance can be delivered at scale without relying on one-to-one coaching
	•	validate product-market fit across a broader user base

I am particularly interested in exploring how AI can support decision-based learning, where users are guided through complex, real-world processes rather than simply consuming content.

The programme would provide valuable support in developing the product architecture, strengthening the EdTech positioning, and accelerating the transition from a service-based model to a scalable platform.
ai_problem_solving:
AI enables JD Mastery to deliver personalised, high-quality career guidance at scale, which would not be possible through traditional coaching alone.

The core challenge in job search is not access to information, but the ability to translate that information into clear, context-specific decisions. Each user requires guidance that is tailored to their background, goals, and current stage in the job search process.

Traditionally, this level of personalised support can only be delivered through intensive one-to-one coaching, which is inherently limited in scale and accessibility.

AI allows us to operationalise this process by:
	•	analysing user inputs (background, goals, progress)
	•	generating structured guidance for positioning, outreach, and communication
	•	providing iterative feedback at each stage of the workflow

This transforms a previously human-dependent process into a scalable system, while maintaining a high level of personalisation.

Without AI, the solution would remain a high-touch, low-scale service model, limiting its reach and impact.
ai_team_expertise:
The team combines strong domain expertise in career strategy with technical capability in AI-enabled product development.

The founder, Johanna Wang, brings experience from management consulting (KPMG, Accenture) and has worked directly with over 2,000 students. This provides deep insight into user behaviour, decision-making patterns, and common failure points in the job search process.

On the technical side, the product is supported by a part-time technical co-founder with a background in Computer Science, responsible for building the platform architecture and integrating AI-assisted workflows.

Our approach to AI is application-focused rather than research-driven. We leverage existing large language models and focus on designing structured workflows, prompts, and feedback systems that translate domain expertise into scalable user guidance.

This combination of domain knowledge and technical implementation allows us to build a practical, user-centred AI product.
